evidence: Runtime-scoping helpers are redefined with different semantics across modules (`backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts`, `backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/uploads.ts`, `backend/src/workers/voicebot/handlers/transcribeHandler.ts`) rather than centralized behind one boundary.
Voice session lifecycle spans route/controller + worker + dedup/service paths, with shared filtering assumptions duplicated rather than composed through one module contract.
cross_module_architecture.issues_preventing_higher_score: Dependency direction is mostly service-oriented, but runtime scoping and session-access behavior still leak across route and worker layers.

evidence: `backend/src/api/routes/crm/tickets.ts:208` accepts both `statuses` and misspelled `satuses`, creating a permanent naming outlier in active API input.
Voicebot route surfaces mix legacy underscore naming (`/create_session`, `/close_session`) and newer flattened naming (`/upload_audio`, `/session_done`) across route modules.
`backend/src/api/routes/crm/tickets.ts:272` swallows enrichment failures with `catch {}` while nearby handlers return hard failures, producing inconsistent failure semantics in one module.
CRM routes return raw `String(error)` payloads (`tickets.ts:289,346,417`) while FinOps routes use structured `AppError` + `sendOk` envelope (`backend/src/api/routes/finops/fxRates.ts`).
`app/src/store/projectsStore.ts` fetch actions log and suppress errors, while CRUD actions rethrow; caller behavior is inconsistent by operation type.

`app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts` centralizes many unrelated capabilities (socket lifecycle, upload, taskflow, codex, session CRUD, normalization) behind one giant store interface, reducing abstraction leverage.

`backend/src/workers/voicebot/handlers/codexDeferredReview.ts` co-locates command execution, prompt shaping, DB updates, and Telegram transport in one handler, indicating low-separation abstraction layers.
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Abstractions exist, but key modules are still broad orchestration hubs where interfaces mostly aggregate responsibilities rather than constrain them.

evidence: `app/src/store/permissionsStore.ts` calls endpoints not implemented by backend (`voicebot/permissions/users/additional_roles`, `voicebot/permissions/users/access_level`) and uses wrong path prefix for `getUserPermissions` (`permissions/users/permissions` without `voicebot/`).
`updateUserProjectIds` posts to `voicebot/permissions/users/projects` while backend mutating endpoint is `POST /users/projects/set` (`backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/permissions.ts:449`).
Backend response shapes are inconsistent across adjacent APIs (`res.status(200).json(data)`, `{ ticket }`, `{ result: 'ok'}`, `{ success: true }`), forcing per-call parsing logic in frontend stores.
All CRM ticket write/read endpoints in `backend/src/api/routes/crm/tickets.ts` are mounted without auth/permission middleware.

Sibling API areas enforce auth explicitly (`backend/src/api/routes/finops/fxRates.ts` uses `authMiddleware` and `requireAdmin`; miniapp routes use `requireAuth` in `backend/src/miniapp/routes/index.ts`).
Most reviewed files avoid repetitive restating comments and avoid obvious boilerplate-only wrappers.
Large modules still include some fallback-heavy branches, but they are generally tied to real compatibility constraints rather than pure generated verbosity.
ai_generated_debt.issues_preventing_higher_score: A few broad handlers contain defensive branch accumulation and mixed legacy code paths that increase ceremony.

evidence: Runtime-tag routing remains active in production paths (`backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts`, `backend/src/workers/voicebot/handlers/transcribeHandler.ts`, `backend/src/services/voicebot/voicebotWebmDedup.ts`).
Legacy compatibility aliases remain first-class in active routes (`backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/uploads.ts` keeps `/create_session` and `/close_session` handlers).
Dedicated script still backfills runtime tags (`backend/scripts/runtime-tag-backfill.ts`), indicating migration is still operationally live.

Assigned files show overloaded flat modules: `backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts` (6001 LOC), `app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts` (2016 LOC), `app/src/pages/voice/SessionsListPage.tsx` (1725 LOC).
Single files mix API boundary, domain rules, normalization, side-effect orchestration, and transport concerns, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

Packet `holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles` is empty, so objective fan-in/fan-out metrics are unavailable; direct file-size and mixed-concern evidence from assigned files still indicates structural overload.
Voice domain ownership is blurred between monolithic route/store modules and worker handlers; key flows require understanding multiple large files.
Subsystem decomposition exists by directory, but boundary purity is weakened by broad orchestration files.
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Top-level domains are recognizable, but major modules still act as catch-all entry points.

evidence: Frontend-backend handoffs require per-call exception handling due API shape drift (notably permissions endpoints and heterogeneous response payloads).
Voice session handoffs depend on multi-step glue logic across route/store/worker seams, increasing incidental coordination complexity.
mid_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Boundary seams are functional but still rely on ad-hoc translation and compatibility conditionals.

evidence: `app/src/pages/voice/SessionsListPage.tsx:744` polls every 500ms via `setInterval` while already listening for `voicebot:active-session-updated`, introducing unnecessary periodic sync churn.
LocalStorage read/write blocks and repeated try/catch patterns in one page increase local control-flow noise instead of consolidating persistence concerns.

findings.summary: Frontend permissions store targets endpoints and paths that do not match backend route contract.
evidence: `getUserPermissions` calls `permissions/users/permissions` (missing `voicebot/` prefix) in `permissionsStore.ts:200`.

`updateUserAdditionalRoles` calls `voicebot/permissions/users/additional_roles` and `updateUserAccessLevel` calls `.../users/access_level`, but these handlers are absent from backend permissions routes.
`updateUserProjectIds` posts to `voicebot/permissions/users/projects`, while backend mutating route is `POST /users/projects/set` and `/users/projects` is read-oriented.
findings.suggestion: Define and export a typed permissions API contract (route constants + request/response schemas) shared by backend and frontend. Update `permissionsStore` to call only implemented endpoints (`/users/permission/add|remove`, `/users/projects/set`, `/users/role`), and remove phantom routes.

findings.summary: CRM ticket routes are unauthenticated while sibling domains enforce explicit auth guards.
evidence: All handlers in `crm/tickets.ts` are declared as plain `router.post(..., async ...)` with no auth/permission middleware.
FinOps routes in `fxRates.ts` consistently apply `authMiddleware` (and `requireAdmin` for write).
Miniapp routes apply `requireAuth` before ticket operations (`miniapp/routes/index.ts:178,334,403`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared CRM auth middleware chain (auth + role/permission checks) and apply it at router level (`router.use(...)`) in `crm/tickets.ts`, with explicit per-route permission policies for read/write/comment/work-hours actions.

findings.summary: Runtime-tag compatibility logic is still embedded in active routing and worker queries.
evidence: `sessions.ts` repeatedly applies `buildRuntimeFilter/mergeWithRuntimeFilter` with `field: 'runtime_tag'` in read and write paths.
`transcribeHandler.ts:137-142` wraps queries with runtime-tag filter.
`voicebotWebmDedup.ts:43-55` runtime-scopes both message and session queries by runtime tag.
A dedicated backfill script still writes `runtime_tag: 'prod'` across runtime-scoped collections.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged migration to deployment-level isolation: 1) add a single `runtimeScopeMode` feature flag module, 2) stop writing new `runtime_tag` values, 3) replace query-time runtime-tag filters with DB-instance boundaries, 4) remove `runtime-tag-backfill.ts` after validation on a shadow read pass.

findings.summary: Voice domain is concentrated in oversized multi-concern files that obscure ownership boundaries.
evidence: `sessions.ts` is 6001 LOC and combines session CRUD, taskflow mutation, transcript/categorization editing, rollback, uploads, and summarize triggers.
`voiceBotStore.ts` is 2016 LOC and mixes socket lifecycle, transport, normalization, CRUD, taskflow, and codex paths in one store module.
`codexDeferredReview.ts` combines CLI orchestration, issue-note sync, and Telegram approval-card transport in one handler.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) split `sessions.ts` into `voicebot/sessions/{list.ts,mutations.ts,taskflow.ts,transcript.ts,attachments.ts}` and mount through an index router; 2) split `voiceBotStore.ts` into slice modules (`sessionSlice`, `uploadSlice`, `taskflowSlice`, `socketSlice`); 3) split `codexDeferredReview.ts` into `reviewRunner`, `issueSync`, `telegramNotify` services under `backend/src/services/voicebot/codex/`; 4) update imports with `rg -n "voicebot/sessions|voiceBotStore|codexDeferredReview"` and run `cd backend && npm run build` plus `cd app && npm run build`.

findings.summary: Error behavior varies between silent skip, raw-string failures, and structured AppError flows.
evidence: `crm/tickets.ts:272-274` silently swallows enrichment failures with `catch {}`.
`crm/tickets.ts` returns `res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })` in many handlers, leaking internal error text.
`finops/fxRates.ts` uses typed validation errors (`AppError`) and standardized success envelope (`sendOk`).
`projectsStore.ts` fetch methods suppress errors after logging and only flip `loading`, while CRUD methods rethrow.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a shared error policy: throw typed route errors (or use common middleware) in backend modules, ban empty catches in route handlers, and standardize frontend stores on either `return Result` or `throw` with explicit UI error mapping.

findings.summary: Naming conventions include typo-based API compatibility and mixed route naming styles.
evidence: `crm/tickets.ts:208` keeps misspelled `satuses` in active request parsing.
Voicebot routes concurrently expose underscore (`/create_session`, `/close_session`) and flatter endpoint styles, increasing naming drift across sibling APIs.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate typo input via explicit compatibility shim: parse only `statuses` in main handler, keep `satuses` support in a temporary adapter that logs deprecation metrics and has a removal date. Publish one route naming convention (kebab or snake) for all voicebot endpoints and enforce in route registration.

findings.summary: Session list keeps 500ms polling despite event subscription, adding avoidable local complexity.
evidence: `SessionsListPage.tsx:744` starts `setInterval(syncFromGlobals, 500)` while also registering `voicebot:active-session-updated` listener in same effect.
The page already has persisted UI state primitives in `sessionsUIStore.ts`, but FAB synchronization remains ad-hoc localStorage polling instead of store-driven event updates.
findings.suggestion: Remove interval polling and migrate FAB state sync to a single event/store channel: emit state updates from FAB writer into Zustand (`sessionsUIStore`), subscribe once in `SessionsListPage`, and update `voice-fab-lifecycle` e2e to assert event-driven transitions without timer waits.
